2605.00782.title: GeoContra: From Fluent GIS Code to Verifiable Spatial Analysis with Geography-Grounded Repair
2605.00782.paper_summary: Reliable spatial analysis in GIScience requires preserving coordinate semantics, topology, units, and geographic plausibility. Current LLM-based GIS systems generate fluent scripts but rarely enforce these geographic rules at scale. We present GeoContra, a verification and repair framework for LLM-driven Python GIS workflows. It represents each task as an executable geospatial contract-including natural-language questions, schemas, CRS metadata, expected outputs, spatial predicates, topology, metrics, required operations, and forbidden shortcuts. Generated programs undergo static rule inspection, runtime validation, and semantic verification, with violations fed back into a bounded repair loop. Evaluated on 7,079 real geospatial tasks across 15 Boston-area zones, 9 task families, and 11 open-source models (600 runs each), GeoContra improves spatial correctness on closed models from 47.6% to 77.5% for DeepSeek-V4 and from 57.7% to 81.5% for Kimi-K2.5. Across 11 open models, average correctness rises by 26.6%. GeoContra turns fluent code production into verifiable spatial analysis, catching negative travel times, CRS/field-schema violations, missing predicates, and brittle output casts that otherwise yield executable but geographically invalid results.
2605.00782.paper_summary_zh: 可靠的地理資訊科學空間分析需要保留坐標語義、拓撲、單位和地理合理性。當前基於大型語言模型的地理資訊系統生成流暢的腳本，但很少在大規模上強制執行這些地理規則。我們提出了GeoContra，一個用於LLM驅動的Python GIS工作流程的驗證和修復框架。它將每個任務表示為可執行的地理空間契約，包括自然語言問題、架構、坐標參考系元數據、預期輸出、空間謂詞、拓撲、度量、所需操作和禁止的捷徑。生成的程序經過靜態規則檢查、運行時驗證和語義驗證，違規情況會被反饋到有界修復循環中。在15個波士頓地區區域、9個任務類別和11個開源模型（每個模型600次運行）上評估的7,079個真實地理空間任務中，GeoContra將封閉模型的空間正確率從47.6%提高到77.5%（DeepSeek-V4），從57.7%提高到81.5%（Kimi-K2.5）。在11個開放模型中，平均正確率上升了26.6%。GeoContra將流暢的代碼生成轉變為可驗證的空間分析，捕捉負的旅行時間、坐標參考系/字段架構違規、缺失的謂詞和脆弱的輸出轉換，這些情況否則會產生可執行但在地理上無效的結果。
2605.00782.author: Yinhao Xiao et.al.
2605.00782.authors: Yinhao Xiao, Rongbo Xiao, Yihan Zhang
